title: Python memory list-files primitive decomposition

execplan_profile.projection_rule: canonical_core is authoritative for intent, scope, next action, proof, continuation, and closeout; legacy fields remain compatibility projections.

canonical_core.requested_outcome: Advance #892 by converting memory.list-files from copied generated helper mechanics into a rule-driven composition of common generated Python primitives.
canonical_core.hard_constraints: Do not import memory package runtime internals; do not move memory-specific product policy into command-generation code; generic primitives must be parameterized by command-owned constants and rules; keep generated outputs reproducible from the renderer.
canonical_core.agent_may_decide: Primitive helper signatures, exact rule data shape, and focused validation set.
canonical_core.escalate_when: The command requires product judgment that cannot be represented as declarative rules or would make command-generation package-specific.
canonical_core.next_action: Add rule-driven generated primitives for repo target resolution, payload entry projection, role and strategy classification, version and mode detection, and action report emission; refactor memory.list-files to compose them.
proof_expectations: uv run ruff check affected generator/check/generated files
completion_criteria: memory.list-files generated command contains declarative product constants/rules and composes generated resource primitives for the mechanics.
Static proof checks require primitive composition and reject reintroduced copied helper mechanics for memory.list-files.
Generated outputs are reproducible from command-generation.
Any remaining product-domain boundary is recorded instead of claiming full #892 completion.
